name: Steamed Salmon with Black Bean Sauce
recipeInstructions:
See recipe for Chicken Stock.
If the salmon fillets are thicker than 1/2 to 5/8 inch, carefully cut each horizontally into a neat 4-inch square.
Cut off tops and bottoms of peppers to leave a band about 2 inches wide; remove seeds and ribs. Cut two 4-inch long rectangular pieces from each pepper. Julienne the pieces into 1/8-inch strips, keeping them grouped in neat rectangles.
Place salmon on an oiled steamer tray or plate. Arrange pepper rectangles over the fish, covering each fillet completely.
Sprinkle bamboo shoots, black beans, ginger, and scallions over the fish.
In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, chicken stock, ground white pepper, vegetable oil, and sesame oil. Pour the mixture evenly over the fish.
Steam covered until just cooked through and opaque, 5 to 8 minutes (timing varies according to the thickness of the fish). Carefully transfer to a serving plate, spooning any remaining sauce around, and serve immediately.
